name: Coq Au Vin
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large pot or Dutch oven, brown the bacon until crispy. Remove with a slotted spoon and set aside.
2. Add the chicken to the pot and cook over medium-high heat until golden brown on all sides.
3. Once the chicken is browned, remove it from the pot and set aside. Discard the bacon fat but keep the bacon for later use.
4. In the same pot, sauté the shallots, garlic, parsley, thyme, salt, and pepper until fragrant.
5. Add the white onions and mushrooms to the pot. Cook over medium heat until the vegetables are softened.
6. Pour in the dry white wine and chicken broth. Bring to a simmer.
7. Return the chicken to the pot. Adjust seasoning if needed with additional salt or pepper.
8. Sprinkle the cornstarch mixed with cold water over the sauce to thicken it. Stir continuously until thickened.
9. Add the plum tomato to the pot and stir gently.
10. Cover and reduce heat to low. Cook for an additional 20-30 minutes, allowing flavors to meld and the sauce to simmer gently.
11. Before serving, adjust seasoning once more if needed.
12. Serve hot with crusty bread or over rice. Return any remaining bacon to the pan and serve alongside.
